health.mental_health: In recovery from alcohol use disorder with intermittent anxiety and guilt tied to past relapse; attends peer support meetings and is working on rebuilding trust with family. Sleep is improving but stress spikes around anniversaries and family gatherings.
recent_visits: Primary care follow-up for A1C and blood pressure (last month)
Podiatry visit for diabetic foot exam (2 months ago)
Endocrinology consult to adjust diabetes meds (3 months ago)
Outpatient counseling session focused on relapse prevention and relationship repair (weekly)

finance.debt: Car payment and lingering credit card balances from a period of reduced work during heavy drinking; on a structured repayment plan and trying to rebuild credit after a late-payment streak last year.
recent_transactions: Paid $1,250 toward credit card balance as part of repayment plan
Pharmacy copays for diabetes medications (notably higher since starting semaglutide)
Received commission deposit from a condo sale in Sugar House
Paid $450 for continuing education and license renewal fees

relationships: Adult daughter (Amina Banda) in West Valley City; relationship strained but improving after he started consistent sobriety
Adult son (Malik Banda) in Provo; keeps some distance due to past broken promises
Ex-wife (Linda Banda); limited contact, mostly about family events and boundaries
Sponsor from recovery community (Tom H.), checks in several times a week
Close friend and walking buddy (Rafael Ortiz) from a neighborhood diabetes education class
social.social_media_activity: Uses Facebook sparingly to share real estate listings and occasional family photos; avoids posting about recovery publicly. Keeps Instagram private and mostly follows local community pages and Utah housing market updates.
private_conversations: Apologizing to his daughter for missed birthdays and prior intoxicated phone calls
Discussing relapse triggers (open houses with alcohol, loneliness after work)
Setting boundaries with an old drinking friend who still invites him to bars
Talking with sponsor about cravings and medication-assisted treatment (naltrexone)

schedule.work_hours: Typically 9:30am–6:00pm weekdays with evening showings 1–2 nights/week; weekends vary (often 2–5 hours for open houses).
regular_appointments: Morning blood glucose checks and weekly medication injection routine (Sunday mornings)
upcoming_events: Primary care lab draw for A1C and lipids (next Wednesday 8:15am)
Dinner with daughter and grandkids (next Sunday early evening; alcohol-free restaurant planned)
Brokerage office training on new MLS compliance rules (next Friday 10:00am)
schedule.daily_routine: Starts with a short walk and glucose check, then client calls and listing prep mid-morning. Tries to schedule showings earlier to avoid late-night fatigue, cooks simple low-carb dinners, and blocks evenings for meetings or family calls to stay accountable.

professional.company: Independent agent affiliated with a mid-sized Salt Lake City brokerage (Wasatch Front Realty Group)
projects: Listing a 3-bed home in Millcreek and coordinating staging/photos
Working with first-time buyers targeting condos near Sugar House and downtown
Following up with past clients for referrals while rebuilding a reliable reputation
Completing continuing education on ethics and disclosure requirements
professional.performance_issues: Had a dip in lead follow-through and missed a few morning appointments during a relapse period last year; now uses a stricter calendar system and has a managing broker check-in to ensure consistency and reduce risk.
workplace_relationships: Supportive managing broker who requires documented accountability steps after past reliability issues
A newer agent on the team who competes for the same neighborhoods, creating mild tension
A trusted transaction coordinator who double-checks paperwork to prevent mistakes when he is stressed

hobbies: Volunteering occasionally at community housing workshops
Cooking simple meals and experimenting with low-carb recipes
habits: Tracks blood glucose most mornings and logs meals
Avoids bars and events centered on drinking; brings sparkling water to gatherings
Calls sponsor when cravings spike, especially after a stressful negotiation
Uses a pill organizer and sets phone reminders for medications
Keeps an alcohol-free home and discards promotional wine gifts from clients
lifestyle.living_situation: Rents a modest one-bedroom apartment on the east side of Salt Lake City; chose it for walkability and a quieter environment that supports sobriety. Has been gradually reconnecting with family but is not currently living with a partner.
lifestyle.dietary_preferences: Follows a diabetes-conscious, lower-carb diet (lean proteins, vegetables, limited bread/pasta). Tries to limit late-night snacking and avoids sugary drinks; prioritizes consistent meals to prevent glucose swings.
